text: You are not your grand plans. You are your daily patterns.
author: James Clear

text: All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: In times of change, learners inherit the earth, while the learned find themselves beautifully equipped to deal with a world that no longer exists.
author: Eric Hoffer

text: If we are ever in doubt what to do, it is a good rule to ask ourselves what we shall wish on the morrow that we had done.
author: John Lubbock

text: There's nothing you can cling to. No refuge. Just let go, man.
author: Alan Watts

text: In my whole life, I have known no wise people (over a broad subject matter area) who didn’t read all the time—none, zero.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Incredible things can be done simply if we are committed to making them happen.
author: Sadhguru

text: The first effect of adjusting to other people is that one becomes boring.
author: Elias Canetti

text: I have learned silence from the talkative, toleration from the intolerant, and kindness from the unkind; yet strange, I am ungrateful to these teachers.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: The real art of conversation is not only to say the right thing, in the right place, but, far more difficult still, to leave unsaid the wrong thing at the tempting moment.
author: Dorothy Nevill

text: The Universe began when Nothing saw itself in the mirror.
author: Tor Nørretranders

text: You don't have a right to the cards you believe you should have been dealt with. You have an obligation to play the hell out of the ones you're holding.
author: Cheryl Strayed

text: Most people self-limit their ability to learn. Just read books and talk to people. I didn't study rocket engineering, I picked it up along the way.
author: Elon Musk

text: The real truth, that dare not speak itself, is that no one is in control. Absolutely no one.
author: Terence Mckenna

text: So much has been given to me I have not the time to ponder that which has been denied.
author: Helen Keller

text: See how letting go of the world is peaceful. There's nothing you need to hold on to. There's nothing you need to push away.
author: Buddha

text: It’s no use going back to yesterday, because I was a different person then.
author: Lewis Carroll

text:
Out of the night that covers me,
Black as the pit from pole to pole,
I thank whatever gods may be
For my unconquerable soul.

In the fell clutch of circumstance
I have not winced nor cried aloud.
Under the bludgeonings of chance
My head is bloody, but unbowed.

Beyond this place of wrath and tears
Looms but the Horror of the shade,
And yet the menace of the years
Finds and shall find me unafraid.

It matters not how strait the gate,
How charged with punishments the scroll,
I am the master of my fate,
I am the captain of my soul.
author: William Ernest Henley

text: No faith that is not surrounded by doubt can survive.
author: Elias Canetti

text: Interest is the most important thing in life; happiness is temporary, but interest is continuous.
author: Georgia O’Keeffe

text: When you stop expecting people to be perfect, you can like them for who they are.
author: Donald Miller

text: Do not wish for an easy life. Wish for the strength to endure a difficult one.
author: Bruce Lee

text: The worst lesson that can be taught to a man is to rely upon others and to whine over his sufferings.
author: Teddy Roosevelt

text: If you want to ruin your life, spend it trying to change your spouse's behaviour.
author: Charlie Munger

text: One can choose to go back toward safety or forward toward growth. Growth must be chosen again and again; fear must be overcome again and again.
author: Abraham Maslow

text: To enjoy life, the adventurous state of mind must be grasped and maintained. The essential feature of adventure is that it is a going forward into unknown territory.
author: Agnes Martin

text: Greatness is overrated. It's a form of extremism, and it comes with extreme vices that I have no interest in.
author: Kevin Kelly

text: Beyond a wholesome discipline, be gentle with yourself. You are a child of the universe no less than the trees and the stars; you have a right to be here.
author: Max Ehrmann

text: For both ruin and recovery are from within.
author: Epictetus

text: The truth is not a crystal that can be slipped into one's pocket, but an endless current into which one falls headlong.
author: Robert Musil

text: Someone I loved once gave me a box full of darkness. It took me years to understand that this, too, was a gift.
author: Mary Oliver

text: As you think, so shall you become.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Follow your heart but take your brain with you.
author: Alfred Adler

text: As something, I am merely that thing. As no-thing, I am all things.
author: Douglas Harding

text: Not everything that is faced can be changed, but nothing can be changed that is not faced.
author: James Baldwin

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Will Durant

text: The years teach much which the days never know.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Dying societies accumulate laws like dying men accumulate remedies.
author: Nicolás Gómez Dávila

text: It doesn't matter if the water is cold or warm if you're going to have to wade through it anyway.
author: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin

text: Sitting quietly, doing nothing, spring comes, and the grass grows, by itself.
author: Matsuo Basho

text: To understand and be understood, those are among life's greatest gifts, and every interaction is an opportunity to exchange them.
author: Maria Popova

text: When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.
author: Victor Frankl

text: The truth does not change based on our ability to stomach it.
author: Flannery o'Connor

text: How often in life we complete a task that was beyond the capability of the person who we were when we started it.
author: Robert Brault

text: The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
author: Alan Watts

text: Whether a thought is spoken or not, it is a real thing and has powers of reality.
author: Frank Herbert

text: Nothing diminishes anxiety faster than action.
author: Walter Anderson

text: Beware the barrenness of a busy life.
author: Socrates

text: Foolishness is a twin sister of wisdom.
author: Witold Gombrowicz

text: The task we must set for ourselves is not to feel secure, but to be able to tolerate insecurity.
author: Erich Fromm

text: A pessimist sees the difficulty in every opportunity; an optimist sees the opportunity in every difficulty.
author: Winston Churchill

text:
The modern world is optimized for convenience, not improvement.

The default path is usually the more convenient path. And I get it. Who wants their days to be a pain in the neck? I like sitting in air-conditioned rooms and watching my favorite shows too.

But the body and mind only grow when placed under a stimulus. If you want improvement, you have to choose something different than convenience.

It can be lovely to have a day where you do not push yourself, but it rarely works out well if you have a life where you do not push yourself.
author: James Clear

text: Character consists of what you do on the third and fourth tries.
author: James Michener

text: Distrust all in whom the impulse to punish is powerful.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Every book we read must leave us richer or poorer, sadder or happier, safer or more uncertain, but never intact.
author: Nicolás Gómez Dávila

text: Anything you have imposed on yourself to be unhappy is a concept. So, give it away.
author: W. H. L. Poonja

text: Just set one day’s work in front of the last day’s work. That’s the way it comes out. And that’s the only way it does.
author: John Steinbeck

text: No matter how far you have gone down a wrong road, turn back.
author: Turkish proverb

text:
Trust follows consistency.

The business that delivers a quality product every time earns the customer's trust.

The person in the relationship who shows up reliably — who keeps promises, who responds with steadiness — earns the trust of the other.

The pattern is the proof."
author: James Clear

text: If I know what I shall find, I do not want to find it. Uncertainty is the salt of life.
author: Erwin Chargaff

text: What you say about yourself is forgotten. What you do for others compounds. Serve people well, and their word about you will travel further than yours ever could.
author: Shane Parrish

text: He who makes no mistakes makes no progress.
author: Teddy Roosevelt

text: It is easier to fight for one’s principles than to live up to them.
author: Alfred Adler

text: Whatever inspiration is, it’s born from a continuous "I don’t know."
author: Wisława Szymborska

text: Do you think I count the days ? There is only one day left, always starting over. It is given to us at dawn and taken away from us at dusk.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: When you are sorrowful look again in your heart, and you shall see that in truth you are weeping for that which has been your delight.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: No job is too small. No task is below you.
author: Harrison McCain

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Now is simply now. You are simply you. And tell me, since you want to leave the place where you are, where is it exactly you want to go?
author: Kodo Sawaki

text: You can’t define a person. You can’t sum anyone up. Life is non-summative—it is infinite.
author: James Low

text: One of my hopes is to age into a better mindset. The general trend seems to become less open minded, less resilient, less capable of handling change with ease and flexibility as the years roll by. I hope to grow in the opposite direction. How can my mindset be better today than it was yesterday?
author: James Clear

text: In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: You shouldn’t worry about criticism from someone that you would never ask advice from.
author: Brian Halligan

text: One does what one is; one becomes what one does.
author: Robert Musil

text: Fear is a manipulative emotion that can trick us into living a boring life.
author: Donald Miller

text: Where I was born and where and how I have lived is unimportant. It is what I have done with where I have been that should be of interest.
author: Georgia O’Keeffe

text: When I argue with reality, I lose, but only 100% of the time.
author: Byron Katie

text: A good life is one in which things do not go perfectly, but you find a way to have a good day regardless.
author: James Clear

text: We don't receive wisdom; we must discover it for ourselves after a journey that no one can take for us or spare us.
author: Marcel Proust

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Discoveries, like resources, may well be infinite: the more we discover, the more we are able to discover.
author: Julian Simon

text: If you want real control, drop the illusion of control; let life have you. It does anyway. You're just telling yourself the story of how it doesn't.
author: Byron Katie

text:
One of my favorite lessons I’ve learnt from working with smart people:

Action produces information. If you’re unsure of what to do, just do anything, even if it’s the wrong thing. This will give you information about what you should actually be doing.

Sounds simple on the surface - the hard part is making it part of your every day working process.
author: Brian Armstrong

text: Do not be angry with the rain; it simply does not know how to fall upwards.
author: Vladimir Nabokov

text: If you are patient in one moment of anger, you will escape 100 days of sorrow.
author: Chinese proverb

text: Fear, to a great extent, is born of a story we tell ourselves.
author: Cheryl Strayed

text: The curious are provided with much fun and wisdom long after formal education has ended.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Questions are answered as you move, not before you move. Moving forward with an answer that is partially correct will usually fill in the gaps faster than waiting until you come up with a plan that is perfectly correct.
author: James Clear

text: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
author: Carl Rogers

text: Convictions are more dangerous enemies of truth than lies.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: If you are lonely when you are alone, you are in bad company.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Rudeness is the weak man’s imitation of strength.
author: Eric Hoffer

text: Success is something you attract by the person you become.
author: Jim Rohn

text: You sense far more than you are conscious of. Whether you want to or not.
author: Tor Nørretranders

text: Gratitude is the intention to count your blessings every day, while avoiding, whenever possible, the belief that you need or deserve different circumstances.
author: Timothy Miller

text: You may be deceived if you trust too much, but you’ll live in torment if you don’t trust enough.
author: Frank Crane

text: There is nothing more important to true growth than realizing that you are not the voice of the mind, you are the one who hears it.
author: Michael A. Singer

text: It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit.
author: Harry S. Truman

text: It doesn't matter what others do, unless you decide that it matters to you.
author: Michael A. Singer

text: In wisdom gathered over time I have found that every experience is a form of exploration.
author: Aldous Huxley

text: Learning is a weightless treasure you can always carry easily.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: No amount of anxiety makes any difference to anything that is going to happen.
author: Alan Watts

text: The urge to save humanity is almost always only a false-face for the urge to rule it.
author: H. L. Mencken

text: Wisdom is oftentimes nearer when we stoop than when we soar.
author: William Wordsworth

text: My mission is to celebrate higher things, and that is why God gave me speech and a grateful heart.
author: Hölderlin

text: Man is profoundly dependent on the reflection of himself in another man's soul, be it even the soul of an idiot.
author: Witold Gombrowicz

text: You cannot stay on the summit forever; you have to come down again. So why bother in the first place ? Just this: what is above knows what is below, but what is below dos not know what is above. One climbs, one sees. One descends, one see no longer, but one has seen. There is an art of conducting ourselves in the lower regions by the memory of what one saw higher up. When one can no longer see, one can at least still know.
author: Rene Daumal
